raw_text:
Prism Digital Labs

Manager, Frontend Engineering

LOCATION

San Francisco, CA (Remote-
friendly)

TYPE

Full-time

COMPENSATION

$200,000 - $280,000 USD
annually

TECH STACK

React

TypeScript

Next.js

Tailwind CSS

Vite

Webpack

Web Accessibility (WCAG
2.1)

Core Web Vitals &
Performance Monitoring

Jest & React Testing Library

Figma & Design Systems

About Prism Digital Labs: Prism Digital Labs is a venture-
backed software company specializing in design systems and
performance optimization tools for enterprise customers.
Founded in 2019, we work with Fortune 500 companies and
innovative startups to modernize their digital infrastructure.

Our team of 120+ engineers is distributed across North
America and Europe, building products that help thousands of
developers ship faster, more accessible applications.

We're seeking an experienced Manager of Frontend
Engineering to lead and grow our Frontend Platform team.

You'll oversee a team of 5-7 engineers, setting technical
direction, mentoring talent, and shipping customer-impacting
features. In this role, you'll balance hands-on technical
leadership with people management, ensuring your team
delivers high-quality, performant, and accessible web
experiences. You should have 6+ years of frontend
development experience with at least 2 years managing
engineering teams.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Lead and mentor a team of 5-7 frontend engineers,
providing technical guidance, career development, and
performance feedback

Set technical strategy and architecture for React/TypeScript-
based applications, ensuring best practices in performance
optimization and accessibility (WCAG 2.1)

Own the hiring process, including sourcing, interviewing,
and onboarding new frontend engineers to scale the team

Drive adoption of Next.js and modern build tooling
(Vite/Webpack) across projects, establishing shared
patterns and reusable component libraries

Champion web performance initiatives, establishing metrics
and standards for Core Web Vitals, bundle size, and load
time optimization

Collaborate with Product and Design teams to translate
requirements into technical specifications and coordinate
team capacity planning

Conduct code reviews, enforce accessibility standards
(WCAG compliance), and establish frontend best practices
using TypeScript and Tailwind CSS

Manage team goals, OKRs, and quarterly planning; present
technical roadmaps and progress to leadership

Building accessible, high-performanceweb experiences at scale Identify and remove blockers, advocate for team resources,
and foster a culture of continuous learning and
experimentation

REQUIREMENTS

6+ years of professional frontend engineering experience
with significant production React and TypeScript experience

2+ years of hands-on people management experience,
including hiring, mentoring, and performance evaluation of

direct reports

Deep expertise in modern frontend tooling: Next.js, Vite,
Webpack, and module bundling optimization

Demonstrated expertise in web performance optimization
(Core Web Vitals, lighthouse audits, bundle size analysis)

Strong foundation in web accessibility (WCAG 2.1

compliance, semantic HTML, accessibility testing tools)

Experience leading technical projects, making architectural
decisions, and establishing engineering standards across
teams

BONUS POINTS

Experience building or maintaining design systems and
component libraries at scale

Familiarity with CSS-in-JS or utility-first CSS frameworks
(Tailwind CSS)

Experience with cross-functional collaboration (Product,
Design, DevOps) in a fast-paced environment

Background in mentoring junior engineers and growing
early-career talent

Understanding of SEO best practices and server-side
rendering with Next.js

BENEFITS

Competitive equity package (0.5% - 1.5% depending on
seniority)

Comprehensive health insurance (medical, dental, vision)
with 100% premium coverage for employees

Unlimited PTO with minimum 20-day annual policy plus 10
company holidays

Professional development budget of $5,000/year for
conferences, courses, and certifications

Flexible work arrangements with option for full remote or
hybrid schedule

Parental leave (16 weeks paid for primary caregiver, 8
weeks for secondary)

 Home office setup stipend ($2,000) and annual wellness
budget ($1,500)

Monthly team events, annual company retreat in Tahoe, and

casual work environment

APPLY

Please submit your resume, a cover letter describing your
management philosophy and leadership experience, and links
to your portfolio or GitHub profile to
careers@prismdigitallabs.com with the subject line 'Manager
Frontend Engineering'. We review applications on a rolling
basis. For questions, contact our recruiting team at

recruiting@prismdigitallabs.com.
